claim_boundary: Bounded fixture packet for the first reviewable contract-market proof. This packet does not grant tool authority or prove payment settlement.

artifacts.proof_purpose: Defines the bounded work, deliverables, constraints, trace requirements, and tool requirements as constraints only.
Valid bid from an eligible internal bidder with explicit commitments and no tool dependency.
Valid bid from an eligible bidder that records tool dependency as a requirement rather than authority.
Explains mandatory checks, scorecards, recommendation, and why the selected bid wins.
Records the authorized award transition with actor and authority basis.
Records acceptance by the awarded actor before execution begins.
Shows bounded delegation with explicit parent link, delegated scope, and inherited constraints.
Captures delegated deliverables without collapsing parent review and integration.
Shows the parent contractor integrating delegated output into the parent deliverable.
Records completion with explicit artifact links and authority basis.
Provides reviewer-facing trace references across contract, bid, evaluation, delegation, and completion events.
Seeds the later reviewer summary with scope, participants, rationale, caveats, and residual risk sections.
Records packet identity, claim boundary, validation commands, and non-proving limits.
Shows how tool-mediated work is represented as a requirement and review boundary rather than an execution grant.
